claimSummary: Mesa says its lead service line inventory map is public and that there are no lead containing service lines connecting city mains to customer meters.
reviewerNote: Official Mesa lead and copper page reviewed manually for public map and no-public-lead language.

claimSummary: Mesa says residents who received a letter should review next steps for galvanized or unknown material, use the inventory map to check service line status, and that if no letter was sent no further action is required.
reviewerNote: Official Mesa notice guidance reviewed manually for galvanized-or-unknown next steps and no-letter/no-action language.

claimSummary: Chandler says it has not identified a lead service line, approximately 27604 service lines required verification, and a little over 24000 have already been confirmed as non-lead.
reviewerNote: Official Chandler service line inspection page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Chandler says unknown-status notices were sent in fall 2024 and fall 2025, field verifications continue through summer 2026, and residents receive a door hanger once material confirmation is complete.
reviewerNote: Official Chandler notification guidance reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Toledo Division of Water Distribution
claimSummary: Toledo says its water service inventory map is public, all but about 6000 customer lines have been inspected, and only about 100 known customer-owned lead lines remain.
reviewerNote: Official Toledo lead line replacement page reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Toledo Division of Water Distribution
claimSummary: Toledo says annual notices were mailed to about 50000 customers with confirmed lead, galvanized requiring replacement, or unknown material on either side of the service line, and a replacement letter is sent 45 days before scheduled lead line work.
reviewerNote: Official Toledo annual notice guidance reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Toledo Division of Water Distribution
claimSummary: Toledo says its multi-year lead line replacement program removes city-side and customer-side lead service lines and that customer-side replacements are funded at no cost to the owner.
reviewerNote: Official Toledo lead line replacement program page reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Toledo Division of Water Distribution
claimSummary: Toledo says its childcare facility program is a free replacement path for licensed childcare sites with lead service lines and that customer-owned portions are also replaced if identified as lead.
reviewerNote: Official Toledo childcare replacement program details reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Toledo Division of Water Distribution
claimSummary: Toledo says there is no direct charge to the property owner for replacement, that grants ARPA and state funding support the program, and that replacement letters are sent 45 days before scheduled work.
reviewerNote: Official Toledo cost language reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Akron Water Supply Bureau
claimSummary: Akron says its service line inventory can be viewed online, there are no homeowner-owned private lead service lines in Akron, and galvanized private lines may still need removal in limited cases.
reviewerNote: Official Akron service line inventory page reviewed manually for inventory and private-side lead status.

publisherName: City of Akron Water Supply Bureau
claimSummary: Akron says customers with unknown customer-side material should email the utility for a phone-based photo workflow, the city expects public-side lead replacement to finish by the end of 2025, and the lead page explains exposure-reduction steps.
reviewerNote: Official Akron lead page reviewed manually for unknown verification and end-of-2025 public-side replacement language.
